title: HTTP 200 for error responses — breaks HTTP semantics
expected_feedback: Critical: Error responses return HTTP 200 with a 'success: false' wrapper. This breaks HTTP semantics — clients, proxies, and monitoring tools rely on status codes to distinguish success from failure. CDNs may cache 200 error responses. Client HTTP libraries won't trigger error handlers on 200. Fix: use semantic status codes (404 for not found, 422 for validation error) with a structured error envelope: {error: {code, message, details[], trace_id}}. The 'success' wrapper pattern is a legacy anti-pattern from SOAP/RPC-style APIs.
violated_rule: Error model is consistent and machine-parseable
